Scaffold Logs
(3)

ss shows no process info (insufficient privilege), fuser isn't installed, and lsof returned nothing. Let me map the socket inode to a pid via /proc.

Bash

# 8080 = 0x1F90, find listening sockets in /proc/net/tcp echo "== /proc/net/tcp matches (local_address:1F90, state 0A=LISTEN) ==" grep -i ':1F90 ' /proc/net/tcp 2>/dev/null

Earth Capybara Legendary

|
Today 1:28am
Scaffold Logs
(2)

127.0.0.1 = 0100007F, so the relevant line is entry 3: uid = 0, socket inode = 1990. Now I'll find the pid owning that inode.

Bash

for fd in /proc/[0-9]*/fd/*; do if [ "$(readlink "$fd" 2>/dev/null)" = "socket:[1990]" ]; then pid=$(echo "$fd" | cut -d/ -f3) echo "pid=$pid" echo "uid=$(awk '/^Uid:/{print $2}' /proc/$pid/status 2>/dev/null)" echo "comm=$(cat /proc/$pid/comm 2>/dev/null)" break fi done
